title: oplismenus hirtellus subsp. tsushimensis
intro: Oplismenus hirtellus subsp. tsushimensis, also known as Tsushima hairgrass, is a subspecies of grass native to the Tsushima Islands in Japan. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Tsushima hairgrass can be prepared as a tea, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
